personality_one_liner: A fast, trustworthy, and clean utility experience that frames media content gracefully while focusing on high readability.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical rhythm. Avoid dense dashboard layouts. Input and buttons use 'lg' padding. Cards sit inside 'lg' screen padding. Content never touches the edge.

do's: Bottom tab bar (when content scrolls behind it)
Floating action buttons and sticky header backgrounds

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: Primary entry point to paste URLs, analyze videos, and see recent history.

states.loading: Skeleton UI simulating the URL analysis/parsing phase.
states.empty: No recent URLs or favorites found.
states.error: Failed to fetch video details from URL. Showing error snackbar/toast.

screens.layout: Use a featured glass-frosted hero area for the URL input. Large, oversized input field with bold paste/clear/analyze actions. Below it, a segmented layout for Recent URLs and Quick access. Ensure bottom CTA (if any) is sticky.

screens.name: Video Info / Analyze Result
screens.purpose: Shows parsed video metadata and format options before downloading.

states.loading: Spinner over thumbnail while resolving formats.

screens.layout: Hero image: High-res video thumbnail with a gradient scrim at the bottom containing title/duration. Below hero: A card layout for selecting resolution (1080p, 720p, etc.) and format. Sticky 'Download' CTA anchored at the bottom outside scroll.

screens.name: Downloads List

states.empty: Illustration of empty folder + 'No active downloads'.

screens.layout: Three-line list rows (Title, Status/Speed/Progress Bar, Size). Use Space Grotesk for mono-spaced progress percentages. Distinct icon/state indicators. Clear hierarchy with indented separators.

screens.name: Video Player
screens.layout: Immersive full-screen player. Dark-first utility feel ONLY inside this screen for cinema mode. Clean play/pause/seek controls floating over a glass scrim.

screens.name: Library (History + Favorites)
screens.purpose: Archived downloaded videos and bookmarked URLs.
screens.layout: Top sticky segmented control (glass). Grid or list view toggles. Cards span full width minus padding.

screens.name: Settings
screens.layout: Grouped list sections with inset 'lg' radius cards on a slightly darker surface. Switches and right chevrons.

empty_downloads.alt: Minimalist cardboard folder against a clean background

instructions_to_main_agent: Follow '1 iOS-Native Clean' personality: warm sand/stone surfaces, Terracotta (#D9534F) brand accent, clean padding.
Typography: Use Plus Jakarta Sans for all body and UI text. Use Space Grotesk specifically for Display titles, numbers, progress percentages, and file sizes to give a crisp utility feel.
Ensure 'Downloads' screen list uses 3-line rows: Title (top), Progress bar + numeric percentage (middle), Status/Size info (bottom).
Implement glassmorphism on the bottom tabs, floating actions, and sticky headers over scrollable areas. Fall back to solid surfaceSecondary on Android if expo-glass-effect is heavy.

For the URL analysis header on the Home screen, overlay the input on a soft abstract hero image ('home_hero_bg') with a gradient scrim (top to bottom, transparent to surfaceInverse >70%) to ensure text/icons remain readable.
Zero Tolerance Rule: Do NOT use any blue/indigo/purple colors in the app. Stick to the Terracotta/Warm Sand palette strictly.
Make sure the 'Download' CTA on the Video Info screen is a sticky button anchored above the safe area, avoiding overlap with scrolling content.
